dependencies.name: libxcb
dependencies.reason: The toolkit-neutral X11 adapter directly uses system core XCB for checked connection setup, fixed atom interning, bounded property reads, verified EWMH discovery, and observational root events.

dependencies.name: xcb-randr
dependencies.fallback: When the X server does not provide usable RandR state, the validated core-root rectangle supplies the reduced single-output PD1 topology.
dependencies.reason: The X11 adapter directly links libxcb-randr.so.0 from x11-libs/libxcb for bounded RandR version negotiation, output discovery, primary-output queries, and topology-change event selection.

dependencies.name: xorg-server
dependencies.fallback: A separately controlled X server may run the same transport integration binary.
dependencies.reason: The deterministic integration harness starts Xvfb through its displayfd interface with server reset disabled and exercises transport, property, EWMH, root-event, and standard window-manager request behavior.

dependencies.name: openbox
dependencies.fallback: The Openbox-specific lane is registered as skipped; exact dock properties remain testable under plain Xvfb.
dependencies.reason: The optional isolated-WM test lane verifies that a protocol-identified EWMH window manager applies Prismdrake's dock strut to the current desktop work area.

dependencies.name: xprop
dependencies.fallback: The Openbox-specific lane is registered as skipped rather than using an unbounded startup delay.
dependencies.reason: The isolated-WM harness uses xprop only for a bounded _NET_SUPPORTING_WM_CHECK readiness probe before the XCB integration binary performs independent protocol validation.

unresolved.reason: Supported minimum core libxcb, xcb-randr, Xorg server, Openbox, and xprop versions remain unmeasured; the recorded versions are component observations rather than supported minima.
